timeline.heading: Professional Experience
timeline.description: Product Designer with 5 years of experience in ecommerce, combining user research and product thinking to deliver measurable business outcomes. I define and improve digital experiences by identifying user needs, analyzing behavior, and aligning solutions with business goals and technical constraints. My work focuses on optimizing conversion, product discoverability, and delivery efficiency through iterative design and cross-functional collaboration.

achievements: Led the design and delivery of ecommerce features, balancing user experience, business goals, and technical constraints.
Applied UX analysis to identify friction in key flows (product discovery, checkout), proposing improvements during development cycles.
Implemented generative AI workflows, reducing feature delivery time by 50% while maintaining experience quality.
Coordinated with cross-functional teams to ensure product requirements and UX decisions were correctly implemented.
Contributed to continuous product improvement by identifying blockers, usability issues, and optimization opportunities.
Led redesign initiatives across 15+ ecommerce sites, improving navigation, information architecture, and conversion flows.
Identified user and business opportunities through research and behavioral analysis, translating insights into product improvements.
Reduced pagination by 50% on Onda de Mar, improving product discoverability and user engagement.
Contributed to Kreditime product growth, generating 1,000+ leads and increasing conversion rates by 15-20%.
Facilitated workshops to align stakeholders on user needs, product priorities, and solution direction.
Implemented structured design processes (Double Diamond), improving clarity in decision-making and delivery.
Supported product improvements through usability testing, research insights, and iterative design.
Designed wireframes and prototypes to validate solutions and improve user interaction flows.
Collaborated with product and development teams in agile environments to deliver continuous improvements.
Conducted usability audits to identify friction points and support product optimization.

kreditime.hero_title: Improving credit adoption in ecommerce checkout
kreditime.hero_description: I led the design of a fintech credit experience embedded in ecommerce checkout, focused on increasing user activation and conversion.
kreditime.context: Kreditime is a fintech solution integrated into ecommerce platforms to offer credit at the moment of purchase. Despite user interest, adoption was low due to friction in onboarding and lack of trust in financial interactions.
kreditime.problem: Users dropped off during the credit onboarding process due to complex identity verification requirements, unclear financial terms, and a disconnected experience from the ecommerce checkout flow.
kreditime.role_description: I defined the end-to-end credit experience, conducted research (heatmaps, surveys, interviews), identified friction points, and collaborated with developers to implement improvements within real checkout environments.
kreditime.process_description: We hypothesized that simplifying onboarding, improving financial clarity, and integrating the experience into checkout would increase trust and conversion.
process_steps: Analyze user behavior through heatmaps and drop-off points.
Identify friction in identity verification and onboarding flows.
Iterate solutions based on real usage patterns.

decisions.title: Reduce friction in identity verification flow
decisions.description: Simplified onboarding by removing unnecessary fields, introducing progressive steps, and adding real-time validation feedback to reduce cognitive load.

decisions.title: Increase trust through clarity
decisions.description: Simplified financial language, highlighted key terms (fees, installments, total cost), and improved visual hierarchy to make commitments understandable.

decisions.title: Embed credit into checkout
decisions.description: Integrated Kreditime directly into the ecommerce checkout to reduce context switching and create a seamless experience.

decisions.title: Iterate using behavioral data
decisions.description: Used heatmaps and funnel analysis to identify friction points and continuously optimize the experience.

kreditime.results_description: The redesigned onboarding and checkout integration significantly improved adoption and conversion rates.

metrics.label: Qualified Users
metrics.desc: Users successfully completed onboarding after simplifying the process and reducing friction.

metrics.label: Conversion Rate
metrics.desc: Improved conversion by integrating credit into checkout and reducing drop-off during identity verification.

metrics.label: Identity Verification Abandonment
metrics.desc: Significant reduction in abandonment during identity verification stages.

kreditime.learning_quote: In fintech, trust and clarity directly impact conversion.
kreditime.learning: Financial products require more than usability—they require transparency and confidence. Simplifying processes and aligning the experience with user expectations can significantly increase adoption.

ondademar.hero_title: Optimizing product discovery to increase conversion
ondademar.hero_description: I redesigned product discovery flows to reduce friction and improve conversion in a high-volume ecommerce platform.
ondademar.context: Onda de Mar is a premium swimwear ecommerce brand in LATAM with a large product catalog. Users struggled to find relevant products due to inefficient categorization and excessive navigation steps.
ondademar.problem: Product discovery required too many steps due to poor information architecture and weak filtering systems, leading to high bounce rates and lost sales opportunities.
ondademar.role_description: I led the redesign of product discovery, conducted research, restructured the information architecture, and worked with cross-functional teams to implement scalable solutions.
ondademar.process_description: We hypothesized that simplifying navigation, improving categorization, and introducing better filtering would reduce friction and increase product discoverability and conversion.
process_steps: Audit of 500+ SKUs to identify categorization issues.
Card sorting sessions to understand user mental models.
Analysis of navigation behavior and drop-off points.
Iterative prototyping of filtering and navigation systems.

decisions.title: Simplify information architecture
decisions.description: Reduced unnecessary categories and reorganized taxonomy based on user mental models to improve navigation clarity.

decisions.title: Introduce faceted search and filters
decisions.description: Implemented dynamic filtering (size, color, category) to allow users to narrow results faster and reduce browsing effort.

decisions.title: Reduce navigation depth
decisions.description: Minimized the number of steps required to reach product pages, improving efficiency in product discovery.

decisions.title: Standardize product listing experience
decisions.description: Created consistent UI patterns across listing pages to improve scanning and decision-making.

ondademar.results_description: The redesign significantly improved product discoverability and reduced friction across the browsing experience.

metrics.label: Reduction in navigation steps
metrics.desc: Users required fewer interactions to find products within the catalog.

metrics.label: Increase in sales
metrics.desc: Improved product discovery directly impacted purchase completion.

metrics.label: Category exploration
metrics.desc: Users browsed more categories per session due to improved navigation clarity.

ondademar.learning_quote: Reducing friction in discovery directly impacts conversion.
ondademar.learning: In ecommerce, users don't need more options—they need better paths to find them. Structuring information around user mental models is key to improving both experience and business performance.

malva.hero_description: I designed a scalable product system to unify digital and physical experiences, improving consistency and accelerating product delivery.
malva.context: Malva is a premium fashion brand with 16 physical stores and a growing digital presence. The experience across channels was inconsistent, with no unified design language or scalable system to support growth.
malva.problem: The lack of a unified system caused inconsistencies between physical and digital experiences, slowed down product development, and made it difficult to scale new features across platforms.
malva.role_description: I led the definition of a scalable design system, facilitated alignment between stakeholders, and ensured the system could support both digital and physical touchpoints.
malva.process_description: We hypothesized that creating a unified design system aligned with brand and operational needs would improve consistency, reduce design and development time, and enable scalable product growth.
process_steps: Conducted field research in physical stores to understand the brand experience.
Audited existing digital assets to identify inconsistencies.
Defined design principles aligned with brand identity and user expectations.
Built a scalable system using design tokens and reusable components.

decisions.title: Define a unified design language
decisions.description: Created a consistent visual and interaction system to align digital platforms with the in-store brand experience.

decisions.title: Build a scalable component system
decisions.description: Developed reusable components and design tokens to standardize UI and accelerate future product development.

decisions.title: Align system with operational needs
decisions.description: Ensured the system worked across multiple teams (design, development, marketing), reducing friction in implementation.

decisions.title: Enable omnichannel consistency
decisions.description: Connected digital and physical touchpoints to create a seamless and recognizable brand experience.

malva.results_description: The implementation of the design system improved consistency, accelerated delivery, and enabled scalable product development.

metrics.label: Stores aligned
metrics.desc: Unified brand experience across all physical and digital channels.

metrics.label: Faster delivery
metrics.desc: Reduced design and development time through reusable components.

malva.learning_quote: Systems are not just design tools—they are product enablers.
malva.learning: A design system is not only about visual consistency, but about enabling teams to move faster and build scalable products. Aligning design decisions with operational needs is key to long-term product success.

caseStudyLabels.wanna_know_more: Want to know more about this project?
caseStudyLabels.decisions_default: We prioritized functional experience over cluttered visual solutions.
caseStudyLabels.learning_default: In design, clarity is the bridge to conversion.
